q: Large multicellular animals have evolved transport system as organisms get larger and have higher metabolic rates.
In many large animals, circulatory system is in the form of mass transport system.
Mass transport system is an arrangement of structures by which substances are transported in the flow of a fluid.
Single-celled organisms have a low surface area to volume ratio.
In large multicellular organisms, the surface area to volume ratio is low.
Many chemical reactions take place inside every microscopic cell in multicellular animals.
Respiration supplies energy for the other reactions of life, but it also produces the toxic waste product carbon dioxide.
Mass transport systems have exchange surfaces to get materials into and out of the transport system.
A system of vessels that carry substances are usually tubes, sometimes following a very specific route.
Mass transport systems must have a way of making sure that substances are moved in the right direction.
Moving materials fast enough may involve mechanical methods such as the pumping of the heart.
A suitable transport medium (e.g., fluid) is a feature of most mass transport systems.
